description: Two adapters negotiate different KANN capability sets. Both satisfy the unconditional MUST contract identically (Invariant 1: the Controller uses only generic verbs; worker_kind is opaque). Missing KANN capabilities degrade OBSERVABILITY/control fidelity only (pause -> boundary freeze; no events.stream -> worker.raw folding), never the security floor (Invariant 9).
conventions: canonical proto3 JSON; each interaction lists the expected response per profile. Seeds/isolation identical to golden-run and elided as $SEED/$ISOLATION.

must_only_observational.degradation: controller polls State and folds stdout captured as worker.raw; semantic events (worker.message/tool_call/tool_result) are ABSENT

assertions.assert: both profiles pass the identical MUST suite (Create/Start/Signal(CANCEL)/State/Reap/Artifacts) with identical LifecycleStatus progression and identical terminal state; worker_kind ('echo-stub' vs 'claude-code') is opaque and MUST NOT change which verbs the Controller issues
SHIM_SIGNAL_CANCEL succeeds on both profiles regardless of declared capabilities (it is the only unconditional MUST signal); PAUSE/RESUME are KANN (require CAPABILITY_CONTROL_PAUSE_RESUME)

on must_only_observational, Signal(PAUSE) returns ShimSignalResponse.degradedToBoundary == true and status.suspensionReason == SUSPENSION_REASON_PAUSED_BY_USER (the paused OVERLAY still holds via boundary freeze); it is a success, not an error, and status.state stays a non-terminal top-state

without CAPABILITY_EVENTS_STREAM, StreamEvents is unavailable and observability degrades to worker.raw-only folding; semantic events are ABSENT for must_only yet the run reaches the SAME terminal state and a non-empty Artifact-Manifest as fully_cooperative

the security floor is identical across tiers: the IsolationContract passed to Create (profile, egress.defaultAction == EGRESS_ACTION_DENY, resourceLimits, imageTrust) and the redaction/seed inputs are byte-identical for both profiles; a missing capability degrades observability/control fidelity ONLY and never lowers the floor
NegotiateResponse.workerKind never appears in any Controller control-flow branch (grep-gate): no worker-specific symbol drives lifecycle decisions
